name: Tala
description: Tala, in Filipino mythology, is the goddess of the stars. Her name directly translates to 'star' in Tagalog and other Philippine languages. She is often depicted as a beautiful and radiant being, responsible for bringing light to the night sky. In some myths, Tala is the daughter of the supreme god Bathala and a mortal woman, making her a demigoddess. Her primary role is to guide travelers and seafarers during the night, ensuring their safe passage. She is also associated with beauty, guidance, and hope. Different regions and ethnic groups across the Philippines have slightly varying interpretations of Tala's role and origin. For example, some versions depict her as one of the daughters of the sun god, fighting against darkness. Other narratives describe her as a companion or guide to the moon goddess, Bulan. Regardless of the specific story, Tala remains a significant figure representing light and direction in the rich tapestry of Filipino mythology. She embodies the celestial beauty and serves as a reminder of the guiding presence in the vastness of the night sky. She is a symbol of hope and navigation, reminding people to look up and find their way even in the darkest of times. Her presence in the cosmos is a testament to the power of light and the enduring spirit of guidance.
subtitle: Goddess of the Stars, Guiding Light

keyMyths.title: Tala as Daughter of Bathala
keyMyths.description: Tala is said to be the daughter of Bathala, the supreme god, and a mortal woman, granting her divine powers.

keyMyths.title: Tala Guiding Travelers
keyMyths.description: Tala's light guides travelers and seafarers, ensuring their safe journey through the night.

keyMyths.title: Tala and the Moon Goddess
keyMyths.description: Some myths portray Tala as a companion or guide to the moon goddess Bulan, working together to illuminate the night.

sources.author: Jordan Clark
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of deities and myths in the Philippines.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of deities and myths in the Philippines.
